channel.title: Stewart Masters
channel.description: Operator perspectives on AI adoption, digital operations, and product execution inside real businesses.

item.title: The AI Problem Is Adoption, Not Capability, Stewart Masters
item.description: The tools are good enough. The bottleneck is people, process, and organisational readiness. Here's why most AI implementations stall at adoption.

item.title: The Board's Role in a Cybersecurity Incident
item.description: A cybersecurity incident is a board-level event. Most boards aren't ready. Here's what governance looks like before, during, and after.

item.title: How to Scale Culture Across Multiple Markets
item.description: Culture doesn't scale automatically. What works in one market can break in another. Here's how to maintain coherence without imposing uniformity.

item.title: When Advisory Boards Actually Work
item.description: Most advisory boards are a list of names on a deck. Here's what separates the ones that actually deliver from the ones that sit in meetings and disengage.

item.title: The Founder's Guide to Stepping Back from Product
item.description: At some point, every founder has to stop being the de facto CPO. Most do it too late and too badly. Here's how to make the transition well.

item.title: Why Most AI Projects Fail in Companies | Stewart Masters
item.description: Most AI projects don't fail because the technology doesn't work. They fail because of decisions made before a single line of code is written.

item.title: How to Evaluate a CTO, Stewart Masters
item.description: A CTO who built your first product may not be right for your next stage. Here's how to evaluate technical leadership honestly.

item.title: Why Most Professionals Don't Know Their Market Value
item.description: Most professionals are rigorous with data at work and completely blind about their own finances. Here's why that gap exists and how to close it.

item.title: Why Your AI Strategy Needs a Data Strategy First
item.description: Most AI projects fail not because of the model, but because the data isn't ready. Here's why you need the data strategy before the AI strategy.

item.title: How to Structure a Digital Transformation Budget
item.description: Most digital transformation budgets are built around tools and projects, not outcomes. Here's how to structure one that's tied to what you're trying to change.

item.title: The NED's Guide to Understanding Technology, Stewart Masters
item.description: You don't need to be a technologist to be an effective NED on a tech-led business. You need to ask the right questions. Here's how.

item.title: How to Measure Product-Market Fit Honestly, Stewart Masters
item.description: Product-market fit is real, but most teams don't know when they have it. Here's how to look past vanity signals and measure it honestly.

item.title: When to Build vs. Buy in a Digital Business, Stewart Masters
item.description: The build vs. buy decision is never just technical. It's strategic. Here's how to make it without regretting it six months later.

item.title: How Boards Should Evaluate AI Risk, Stewart Masters
item.description: Boards are being asked to govern AI without the frameworks to do it. Here's a practical approach to evaluating AI risk at board level.

item.title: Data Strategy for Non-Technical Founders, Stewart Masters
item.description: You don't need to be technical to make good decisions about data. You just need to ask the right questions and avoid the most expensive mistakes.

item.title: Why Most Product Roadmaps Are Fiction, Stewart Masters
item.description: Roadmaps feel like planning. They're usually something else. Here's why most product roadmaps fail to reflect reality, and what to do instead.

item.title: How to Hire Your First Head of AI, Stewart Masters
item.description: Most companies hiring for AI leadership are asking the wrong questions. Here's what the role actually requires, and how to find someone who can do it.

item.title: The Real Cost of Tech Debt in a Growing Business
item.description: Tech debt isn't just a developer problem. It's a leadership and growth problem. Here's how it actually slows businesses down, and what to do about it.

item.title: OKRs Without the Bureaucracy, Stewart Masters
item.description: OKRs work. The way most companies implement them doesn't. Here's how to get the benefits without the bureaucracy.

item.title: What Data Governance Actually Means in Practice
item.description: Data governance sounds like compliance theatre. Done properly, it's how organisations stop flying blind. Here's what it actually means in practice.

item.title: The Case for a Chief AI Officer, and When Not to Hire One
item.description: The Chief AI Officer is becoming a fixture in leadership teams. But it's the wrong hire for most companies. Here's how to tell if you need one.

item.title: Why Technology Projects Fail at Handover, and How to Fix It
item.description: Most technology projects succeed in build and fail in handover. Here's what the gap actually consists of, and how to close it.

item.title: How to Structure Your First 90 Days as a NED
item.description: The first 90 days as a NED set the tone for your entire tenure. Here's how to structure them so you're genuinely useful from day one.

item.title: What Good Looks Like for an AI-Ready Board, Stewart Masters
item.description: Most boards are unprepared for the AI decisions landing on their agendas. Here's what an AI-ready board actually looks like.

item.title: KPIs vs Metrics: Why the Difference Matters
item.description: Most organisations drown in metrics while blind to performance. Here's the difference between a KPI and a metric, and why it changes how you manage.

item.title: How to Write an AI Business Case That Gets Approved
item.description: Most AI business cases are rejected not because the AI doesn't work, but because the case is built wrong. Here's how to build one that gets approved.

item.title: How to run a board meeting that actually matters
item.description: Most board meetings are status updates with governance theatre. Here's how to structure one where the right decisions get made by the right people.

item.title: What digital-first operations actually means
item.description: Digital-first operations isn't about having more software. It's about designing processes where the digital layer drives the outcome, not just records it.

item.title: Why most companies automate the wrong things first
item.description: Most companies start with the visible, easy processes and leave the high-value decisions untouched. Here's how to choose what's actually worth automating.

item.title: The difference between a roadmap and a plan, Stewart Masters
item.description: A roadmap tells you where you're going. A plan tells you how to get there. Most teams have one and call it the other, and it costs them dearly.

item.title: How to hire a product manager who actually builds
item.description: Most PM hiring processes select for people who present well, not people who ship. Here's how to hire a product manager who actually builds.

item.title: What I learned from 20 years of bad strategy decks
item.description: After two decades of bad strategy decks, the same patterns keep appearing. Here's what separates one that works from one that doesn't.

item.title: Why your CTO and CMO don't speak the same language
item.description: CTOs think in systems. CMOs think in stories. The gap between them is where roadmaps stall, data gets misread, and AI projects die in committee.

item.title: The Ops Stack Every Digital Business Needs
item.description: Most digital businesses run on 15 disconnected tools and call it an ops stack. Here's what the stack should actually look like — and why it matters.

item.title: What makes a great product brief, and why most teams skip it
item.description: A product brief isn't a spec doc. It's a contract between what the business needs and what the team is building. Most teams skip it and pay for it later.

item.title: How to Think About AI ROI
item.description: Most AI ROI frameworks measure the wrong things. Here's a better way to think about value that actually holds up inside a real business.

item.title: What Is Agentic AI? A Guide for Business Leaders
item.description: Agentic AI systems plan, decide, and take actions autonomously. Here's what that means for business leaders — and what it changes.

item.title: What Boards Need to Know About Shadow AI Right Now
item.description: Shadow AI — employees using AI tools without corporate approval — is already happening inside your organisation. Here's what boards need to know.

item.title: Three Digital Transformations That Failed — and Why
item.description: Most digital transformation failures share the same causes. Drawing on three I've seen up close, here's what actually went wrong.

item.title: Digitisation vs Digitalisation: What Is the Difference?
item.description: Digitisation, digitalisation, and digital transformation are not the same. Here's the difference that actually matters for business leaders.

item.title: What Is a Fractional Leader, and When Does It Make Sense?
item.description: Fractional leadership is growing fast, but it's not right for every situation. Here's how to decide whether it makes sense for yours.

item.title: What Is a Non-Executive Director?
item.description: What is a Non-Executive Director, what do they actually do, and when does your business actually need one? A practical guide.

item.title: What Is AI Fluency, and Why Every Executive Needs It in 2026
item.description: AI fluency isn't about learning to code. It's about knowing enough to lead well in a world where AI is embedded in how work gets done.

item.title: Board Advisor vs Non-Executive Director
item.description: Board advisors and NEDs are often confused — but the role, accountability, and expectations are very different. Here's what actually separates them.

item.title: How to Land Your First Board Advisor Role, Stewart Masters
item.description: Most people wait to be discovered. Here's the practical approach to landing your first board advisor role — without waiting to be asked.

item.title: What Is a Digital Strategy?
item.description: Most organisations think they have a digital strategy. What they have is a technology plan. Here's what an actual digital strategy looks like.
